title: Eat a Peach
author: David Chang
description: A raw, honest memoir from the founder of Momofuku, exploring his journey through the culinary world, mental health struggles, and the meaning of success.

title: Ring Shout
author: P. Djèlí Clark
description: A dark fantasy novella set in 1915 Georgia, where resistance fighters battle the Ku Klux Klan—who are actually literal monsters from another dimension.

title: Apple: Skin to the Core
author: Eric Gansworth
description: A powerful memoir in verse and images about growing up Onondaga among Tuscaroras, exploring identity, family history, and Native American heritage.

title: Over the Woodward Wall
author: Deborah Baker
description: Two children from very different backgrounds find a way over a wall and into the Up-and-Under, a world of impossible wonders and hidden dangers.

title: The Silent Forest
author: Elena Rossi
description: A gripping mystery set in the deep woods of the Pacific Northwest, where an investigator must face her past to solve a disappearance.

title: Mastering React
author: David Chen
description: The ultimate guide for developers looking to scale their applications with advanced hooks, design patterns, and performance optimization.

title: Quantum Realms
author: Dr. Sarah Vance
description: An accessible yet deep dive into the strange world of subatomic particles and the future of quantum computing and physics.

title: The Digital Age
author: Marcus Thorne
description: An exploration of how modern technology is reshaping our social interactions, privacy, and global economy in the 21st century.

title: Lost in Time
author: Julia Hart
description: A romantic adventure featuring a historian who accidentally triggers an ancient device and wakes up in Victorian London.

title: AI Revolution
author: Alan Turing Jr.
description: A comprehensive look at the rise of large language models and the ethical implications of creating machines that think like humans.

title: Life on Mars
author: Neil Armstrong
description: A scientifically-grounded vision of what humanity's first permanent colony on the Red Planet might look like within the next fifty years.

title: The Last Kingdom
author: Bernard Cornwell
description: The epic story of Uhtred, a Saxon boy kidnapped by Danes, who must choose between his ancestral home and the people who raised him.

title: Coding the Future
author: Grace Hopper
description: A masterclass in clean architecture and logic, teaching developers how to write code that survives the test of time and hardware changes.

title: The Brain Secret
author: Lisa Cuddy
description: Neuroscience meets self-help in this guide to unlocking cognitive potential through better sleep, nutrition, and mental exercise.

title: Mountain Peaks
author: Robert Frost
description: A collection of modern poetry celebrating the rugged beauty of the wilderness and the isolation of the high summits.

title: Data Science 101
author: Python Master
description: Learn to analyze, visualize, and model data using Python. Perfect for beginners entering the world of machine learning.

title: Neon Dreams
author: Case Gibson
description: A cyberpunk thriller following a freelance hacker in a rain-soaked metropolis controlled by mega-corporations.

title: The Glass Hotel
author: Emily St. John
description: A story of money, ghosts, and the collapse of a massive Ponzi scheme that links lives from a remote hotel to the Manhattan elite.

title: Midnight Sun
author: Stephenie Meyer
description: The story of Twilight retold from Edward Cullen's perspective, providing a darker and more complex view of the immortal life.

title: Deep Sea Secrets
author: Jacques Cousteau
description: Unveiling the mysteries of the abyss, this book showcases the bizarre creatures and hidden ecosystems that exist miles below the surface.

title: Empire of Pain
author: Patrick Keefe
description: A devastating portrait of the three generations of the Sackler family and their roles in the opioid crisis.

title: The Great Reset
author: Klaus Schwab
description: A controversial proposal for a post-pandemic world order centered on sustainability, stakeholder capitalism, and global cooperation.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: The definitive guide to breaking bad behaviors and adopting good ones by focusing on tiny, 1% improvements every single day.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut wakes up on a spacecraft with no memory and must use science and logic to save humanity from an extinction-level threat.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library where every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
description: A bold reimagining of the myth of Circe, the daughter of Helios, who turns men into pigs and must stand against the gods of Olympus.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A bold narrative of humanity's creation and evolution—exploring how biology and history have defined us since the Stone Age.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: The allegorical story of Santiago, a shepherd boy on a journey to the Egyptian pyramids in search of a treasure buried in his dreams.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of the boy Paul Atreides and his struggle to control the universe's most vital resource: the spice.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: The Galactic Empire is dying. To preserve knowledge and shorten the coming dark age, one man creates a Foundation of scientists on the edge of the galaxy.
